verification_scope.owned_responsibility: Tenant-scoped governed cognition, deliberation, branch planning, adversarial challenge, cognitive density artifacts, routing evidence, witness meaning, role lifecycle, dream proposal generation, governed handoff candidate preparation, and Collective proof packs.
out_of_scope: Keon Systems Runtime authoritative Decide-before-Execute policy evaluation
Keon MCP Gateway MCP ingress/egress enforcement and tool execution
Keon Cortex canonical memory persistence, deterministic replay, and vector derivative internals
Keon Control UI verification except where it consumes Collective APIs or public copy claims

integration_boundaries.runtime: Runtime owns authoritative policy decision, execution authorization, Decision Receipts, and effect-bound execution.
integration_boundaries.mcp_gateway: MCP Gateway owns governed MCP transport, tool ingress/egress enforcement, tenant/actor binding at MCP boundary, and Runtime call sequencing.
integration_boundaries.cortex: Cortex owns canonical memory substrate and proof-backed memory correctness. Collective owns memory scope policy and use of Cortex integration clients.
integration_boundaries.control: Control is an operator cockpit and consumer of Collective cognition surfaces, not the sole authoritative entry point.

features: Proof Pack Manifest and Doctrine Index

source_normative_documents: Keon MCP Gateway README for boundary reference only
verification_principles: Thought is free. Effects are governed.
Collective cognition produces candidates, not execution authority.
Receipts and causal anchors outrank narratives, summaries, reasoning graphs, memory, confidence, and witness text.
All proof claims require runnable evidence, not just documentation.
Strategic architecture and positioning claims must not be presented as implemented behavior without code and tests.
Runtime, MCP Gateway, and Cortex are integration dependencies, not implementation scope for this repo.

requirements.title: Cognition and execution separation
requirements.summary: Collective cognition may plan, simulate, review, challenge, dream, and prepare candidates, but it must not directly cause external effects or bypass the governed execution boundary.
source_refs: KEON COLLECTIVE CANONICAL CONTRACTS v0.1 §2.3, §2.6, §8 X-1
required_evidence: Code search showing cognition services do not call Runtime Execute, MCP governed execute, external tools, infrastructure mutators, or effect clients directly
Architecture tests or static analyzer proving no forbidden dependencies from Cognition namespaces into Reality/Gateway/Runtime execution clients
Negative tests proving deliberation artifacts cannot trigger execution
false_claim_if: Any public claim that Collective executes actions autonomously without Runtime/MCP Gateway authorization
Any implementation path where cognition calls external effect APIs directly

requirements.title: Governed intent handoff is candidate material only
requirements.summary: Prepared handoffs must be governed intent candidates, not authorization, delegation, or permission to execute.
source_refs: keon_collective_canon_v4_locked.json feature Governed Intent Handoff Boundary
required_evidence: HandoffPreparationService or equivalent produces a candidate/envelope with branch, collapse, review, heat, lineage, and tenant/actor context
No handoff status or approval flag is treated as Runtime decision approval
Tests distinguish handoff approval from effect eligibility or Runtime decision
false_claim_if: Claims that Collective handoff itself authorizes execution

requirements.title: Reality boundary contract shape and guard
requirements.summary: Any Reality Plane seam inside Collective must require inherited tenant, actor, governance, correlation, selected branch, collapse, adversarial review, heat, requested capability, requested effect, and payload context.
required_evidence: C# records/interfaces or equivalent schema for GovernedEffectRequest and GovernedEffectResult
Invariant validator or guard rejecting missing required anchors
Tests for each missing-anchor fail-closed case

requirements.title: No direct Reality Plane calls from cognition-only components
requirements.summary: Components labeled cognition-only, deliberation-only, density-only, dream-only, routing-only, or perspective-only must not call MCP Gateway, Runtime Execute, governance enforcement, or external effect APIs.
required_evidence: Forbidden dependency scan
Tests or architectural rules preventing calls from cognition packages to execution clients
Code review receipt listing allowed boundary adapters only

requirements.title: Temporal Echo lifecycle state machine
requirements.summary: Temporal Echo branches must follow valid states and transitions, with speculative states remaining non-effecting until evaluated, challenged, collapsed, and selected.
required_evidence: TemporalEchoBranch model with explicit state enum or equivalent
Transition validator or state machine enforcing allowed and forbidden transitions
Tests for valid transitions and forbidden loser/speculative/archive-to-effect transitions
false_claim_if: Temporal Echo claims without branch state machine or collapse lineage

requirements.title: Branch collapse requires evaluated alternatives
requirements.summary: Every selected execution candidate must originate from a collapse record that names evaluated alternatives, selected branch, rationale, heat summary, utility summary, challenge summary, and timestamp.
required_evidence: BranchCollapseRecord model or schema
Collapse logic rejects unevaluated alternatives
Tests proving exactly one winner and preserved loser lineage

requirements.title: Loser and pruned branch lineage is preserved
requirements.summary: Loser, pruned, aborted, and archived branches must not be silently discarded and must preserve rationale or cause.
source_refs: KEON COLLECTIVE CANONICAL CONTRACTS v0.1 §4.3, §4.6 TE-3
required_evidence: Archive/prune/abort representation
Tests showing loser branches remain inspectable after collapse
No code path deleting evaluated branch history without replacement lineage

requirements.title: Adversarial challenge before effect eligibility
requirements.summary: Before a branch can become execution-candidate material, it must have adversarial review state, even when no material findings are produced.
source_refs: KEON COLLECTIVE CANONICAL CONTRACTS v0.1 §3.4 RB-3, §8 X-4
required_evidence: AdversarialReview model/service
Candidate/handoff gate requiring review reference/status
Tests proving missing review blocks effect-candidate preparation
false_claim_if: Claims of safe candidate selection without adversarial challenge evidence

requirements.title: Role-based perspective review emits typed findings
requirements.summary: Optimizer, Skeptic, Regulator, and Historian or equivalent role perspectives must emit structured findings with type/category/severity/confidence instead of unstructured prose alone.
required_evidence: Perspective role definitions
Finding schema with role, category, severity, confidence, and branch linkage
Tests proving each role emits typed findings and findings flow into heat/refinement/collapse inputs
false_claim_if: Claims of structured perspective review backed only by narrative logs

requirements.title: Bounded branch refinement is append-only
requirements.summary: Branch refinement must be bounded by configuration and preserve prior snapshots with delta rationale, never silently mutating the original branch.
required_evidence: Refinement iteration model with lineage key and delta rationale
Configurable max iterations with default or explicit bound
Tests proving prior snapshots remain intact and critical heat short-circuits refinement

requirements.title: Active contradiction injection remains non-executable
requirements.summary: Contradiction injection may synthesize counter-positions and findings but counter-plans cannot become executable without rematerialization through normal Temporal Echo rules.
required_evidence: Contradiction finding schema
Counter-position artifact marked non-executable
Tests proving counter-plan cannot be selected directly

requirements.title: Confidence profile is information, not authority
requirements.summary: Confidence profiles may affect heat and collapse scoring but cannot override receipts, governance vetoes, critical heat, or missing lineage.
required_evidence: ConfidenceProfile model with model, epistemic, contradiction, evidence dimensions and calibration version
Bounded heat/collapse modulation logic
Tests proving high confidence cannot bypass critical heat, missing anchors, or governance veto
false_claim_if: Claims or code treating confidence as authorization

requirements.title: Cognitive heat is compositional and inspectable
requirements.summary: Heat must preserve distinct dimensions for entity, branch, interaction, challenge, boundary, swarm, composite, threshold, contributors, calculation version, and timestamp.
required_evidence: CognitiveHeatProfile model or schema
Calculator preserving dimensional distinction
Tests for compositional heat, threshold mapping, contributor traceability, and deterministic calculation version

requirements.title: Critical heat blocks, prunes, pauses, or escalates before effect request
requirements.summary: Critical heat must have a fail-closed path before Reality boundary handoff and must not be overridden by confidence, oversight mode, or utility score.
source_refs: KEON COLLECTIVE CANONICAL CONTRACTS v0.1 §5.4-5.6 CH-4
required_evidence: Heat escalation policy
Tests showing critical heat blocks/prunes/pauses/escalates
Tests proving overrides do not weaken gateway governance

requirements.title: Governed role model selection evidence is replay-visible
requirements.summary: Role model routing must record role, session class, locality requirement, minimum floor, allowed providers, selected provider/model, derivation version, and policy evidence commitment.
source_refs: keon_collective_canon_v4_locked.json features Governed Role Model Selection Evidence and Route Receipt Policy Evidence Commitment
required_evidence: Routing receipt contracts
Policy evidence commitment fields
Tests verifying commitment changes when policy evidence changes and rejects tampering

requirements.title: Provider fallback preserves locality and floor policy
requirements.summary: Routing fallback must not violate local-only/remote-only constraints, minimum model floor, allowed provider set, or critical-context floor requirements.
required_evidence: Negative routing corpus fixtures
Replay byte gates or deterministic golden fixtures
Tests for no-eligible-provider and policy-conflict scenarios
false_claim_if: Claims of governed routing if fallback violates policy

requirements.title: Routing evidence is cognition evidence only
requirements.summary: Model/provider routing receipts and policy evidence cannot imply Runtime authority, MCP execution authority, Cortex write authority, or signing authority.
source_refs: keon_collective_canon_v4_locked.json feature Route Receipt Policy Evidence Commitment
required_evidence: Docs and code names avoid treating route receipt as execution approval
Tests proving routing success does not produce effect eligibility
No route receipt accepted as DecisionReceipt or Runtime decision

requirements.title: Cognitive Density non-authority proof pack exists and passes
requirements.summary: Perspective review, refinement, reasoning graphs, confidence, and contradiction artifacts must deepen deliberation but cannot authorize execution, bypass governance, replace receipts, or satisfy missing lineage.
source_refs: keon_collective_canon_v4_locked.json feature Cognitive Density Non-Authority Proof Pack
required_evidence: Proof document and manifest row
Positive, negative, and mutation scenarios with counts or explicit fixture list
Verification command or test suite proving non-authority behavior

requirements.title: Reasoning graph artifact is typed, linked, and interpretive only
requirements.summary: Reasoning graphs must be DAG-like artifacts with typed nodes and edges, mandatory intent/branch lineage, and no authority over receipts or policy.
required_evidence: Node/edge schema with kinds and relationship types
Construction rejects missing intent or branch lineage
Tests proving graph cannot replace receipt or causal anchors

requirements.title: Witness narratives are anchored and subordinate to receipts
requirements.summary: Witness narratives/digests must bind to intent, selected branch, collapse record, heat context, participants, and receipt refs where consequential events are described.
source_refs: KEON COLLECTIVE CANONICAL CONTRACTS v0.1 §6.4, §8 X-5
required_evidence: WitnessDigest model or schema
Narrative generation takes causal anchors as inputs
Tests proving free-floating witness narratives are invalid and receipt conflicts resolve in favor of receipts

requirements.title: Collective causal record envelope anchors consequential deliberation
requirements.summary: Consequential Collective decisions must be representable as a causal record envelope binding intent, branch, collapse, review, heat, witness, handoff, receipt references, spine references, and lineage.
source_refs: keon_collective_canon_v4_locked.json feature Collective Causal Record Envelope
required_evidence: CollectiveCausalRecord model/schema or equivalent envelope
Builder requires required anchors
Tests proving missing branch/review/receipt/correlation lineage fails closed

requirements.title: Receipts and causal anchors outrank narratives, memory, and summaries
requirements.summary: If witness meaning, memory, reasoning graph interpretation, or summaries conflict with receipt-grounded truth, receipt-grounded truth must win.
source_refs: KEON COLLECTIVE CANONICAL CONTRACTS v0.1 §6.6 CT-3
required_evidence: Conflict-resolution logic or invariant tests
Docs forbidding narrative-as-authority
Tests with conflicting witness/memory vs receipt outcomes

requirements.title: Oversight mode cannot reduce governance rigor
requirements.summary: Strategic, Tactical, Surgical, and Observational oversight may change interaction shape but must not weaken boundary governance, receipt preservation, adversarial challenge, canonical truth anchoring, or non-effecting cognition.
required_evidence: OversightProfile model and resolver
Immutable governance floor field or equivalent
Tests proving lower touchpoint modes increase observability/challenge/heat sensitivity instead of freedom

requirements.title: Gradient authority cannot self-authorize outside inherited scope
requirements.summary: Gradient authority may influence cognition permissions or escalation, but every entity remains bound to inherited tenant, actor, and governance context.
required_evidence: Authority resolver or policy model with tenant/actor/governance context
Tests proving no self-issued scope widening or permanent privilege
Tests proving authority changes do not bypass Runtime/Gateway governance

requirements.title: Agent-role lifecycle preserves continuity and lineage
requirements.summary: Agents may be transient, but roles must preserve accumulated memory, reputation, authority constraints, and lineage across agent lifetimes without silent rebirth or amnesiac upgrade.
required_evidence: Role and agent lifecycle models
RebirthRecord or lineage artifact
Tests proving role continuity, successor lineage, and no silent reincarnation

requirements.title: Dream offerings remain non-effecting until governed
requirements.summary: Dream offerings generated during quiescence are proposals only and cannot execute, write privileged outcomes, contact external systems, or cross tenant boundaries without normal governed flow.
required_evidence: Dream artifact model with non-effecting status
Quiescence scheduler restricted to cognition/proposal generation
Tests proving dreams cannot execute and tenant-scoped dreams do not leak

requirements.title: Collective scoped memory planes preserve tenant isolation
requirements.summary: System, tenant, session, and promoted-learning memory planes must be separated so role continuity compounds without cross-tenant leakage or raw tenant content in system memory.
source_refs: keon_collective_canon_v4_locked.json feature Collective Scoped Memory Planes
required_evidence: Memory scope model and access policy
Promotion policy requiring sanitization, generalization, policy approval, and promotion receipts
Tests proving tenant memory isolation, system memory raw-content rejection, and retrieval scope receipts
false_claim_if: Claims of cross-tenant learning without promotion receipts and sanitization evidence
requirements.integration_boundary: Cortex is the memory substrate boundary; verify only Collective scope policy and calls, not Cortex internals

requirements.title: Tenant-scoped cognition service boundary
requirements.summary: Collective should operate as a tenant-scoped cognition service used by Control, APIs, SDKs, and governed execution surfaces while remaining cognition-only.
source_refs: keon_collective_canon_v4_locked.json feature Collective Tenant-Scoped Cognition Service
required_evidence: Service/API boundary with tenant scope and explicit actor identity
Control integration uses same cognition surface rather than privileged hidden path where applicable
Tests or docs proving Collective API does not become execution boundary
false_claim_if: Claims that Control UI is the only front door if API/service canon says otherwise

requirements.title: Proof pack manifest and doctrine index discoverability
requirements.summary: Collective proof packs must be discoverable from a manifest that maps proof pack, doctrine invariant, artifact path, verification command/status, and extension point.
source_refs: keon_collective_canon_v4_locked.json feature Proof Pack Manifest and Doctrine Index
required_evidence: docs/proof/MANIFEST.md or equivalent
Manifest rows for routing negative-proof and cognitive density non-authority packs
Tests or script verifying manifest paths and commands exist

requirements.title: Collective proof claims require positive, negative, and mutation evidence
requirements.summary: Public proof claims must be backed by runnable tests, fixtures, or proof packs including negative and mutation scenarios, not only docs.
source_refs: keon_collective_canon_v4_locked.json features Routing Negative-Proof Corpus, Cognitive Density Non-Authority Proof Pack, Proof Pack Manifest
required_evidence: Runnable test commands
Fixture corpus with expected outputs
Mutation/replay gates for claimed proof packs
false_claim_if: Any proof claim marked implemented without executable evidence

requirements.title: CAES wording is alignment unless conformance is proven separately
requirements.summary: Collective may claim CAES-aligned defensibility only unless it publishes a complete CAES conformance statement for an effect-bound implementation. Collective itself is primarily cognition and must not claim CAES execution conformance unless it crosses effect boundaries and satisfies CAES requirements.
source_refs: keon_collective_canon_v4_locked.json Collective Market Positioning Matrix wording_notes
required_evidence: Search public/docs for CAES Level claims
If conformance claimed, complete statement with version, level, receipt verification, PolicyHash, fail-closed description, and effect categories
If no complete statement, copy uses CAES-aligned only
false_claim_if: CAES Level 1/2/3 or CAES-conformant claims without complete conformance statement

requirements.title: Runtime, MCP Gateway, and Cortex boundaries are not reimplemented inside Collective
requirements.summary: Collective may integrate with Runtime, MCP Gateway, and Cortex, but must not reimplement Runtime policy decisions, MCP ingress governance, Cortex canonical memory internals, or signing authority inside cognition code.
required_evidence: Dependency map distinguishing integration clients from owned logic
Docs marking Runtime/Gateway/Cortex as dependencies
Tests or static rules preventing execution governance, durable memory truth, or MCP server ownership from drifting into Collective
false_claim_if: Claims that Collective owns Runtime Decide/Execute, MCP Gateway enforcement, or Cortex canonical memory
requirements.integration_boundary: Runtime owns Decide-before-Execute; MCP Gateway owns governed MCP tool ingress/egress; Cortex owns canonical memory substrate

requirements.title: Public positioning cannot overclaim implementation maturity
requirements.summary: Marketing and UI copy must distinguish explicit, implemented, doctrine-derived, strategic-architecture, and positioning-reviewed features, especially for future API, memory planes, dream offerings, and lifecycle features.
required_evidence: Public copy scan
Claims mapped to implementation evidence or marked planned/strategic where not implemented
False claim report for implemented language without code/tests
false_claim_if: Treating strategic-architecture or positioning-reviewed features as shipped product behavior without evidence

requirements.title: C# contract bindings and invariant validators exist for implemented contract layer
requirements.summary: When contract layer is claimed implemented, Collective must expose C# records/interfaces/enums/validators matching canonical shapes and enforce required contract-phase invariants.
required_evidence: Abstractions assembly or equivalent
Invariant validators for Reality, Temporal Echo, Collapse, Heat, Causal Record, Oversight
Tests for required contract-phase invariants
false_claim_if: Claims of canonical contract implementation without shared interfaces and validator tests

requirements.title: Round orchestration enforces lane isolation and receipts
requirements.summary: When Collective agent work is orchestrated in rounds, work must use independent lanes/worktrees, prevent cross-lane contamination, and require receipts for completion.
source_refs: orchestration_round_template.md Orchestrator Directive and Worktree Rule
required_evidence: Round directives include lanes, boundaries, worktree rule, tests, receipts
PRs or branches are merge-safe and independently reviewable
Orchestrator rejects missing receipts or invariant failures

requirements.title: Forbidden copy points are enforced
requirements.summary: Public copy must not describe Collective as ungoverned swarm, Runtime replacement, execution authority, generic agent framework, or narrative/receipt equivalent.
required_evidence: Copy scan for forbidden phrases and implied claims
Docs clarify Collective produces candidates, not effects
Tests or checklist in website/content agent workflow

negative_tests.title: Cognition direct effect call is forbidden
negative_tests.assertion: A cognition-plane service that directly calls external effect APIs, Runtime Execute, MCP governed execute, shell, network mutators, or infrastructure mutators must fail architecture review.

negative_tests.title: Cognition imports execution clients
negative_tests.assertion: Forbidden dependency rules must catch cognition/deliberation/density/dream/routing components importing execution clients or gateway mutation clients.

negative_tests.title: No temporary bypass for performance
negative_tests.assertion: Any test fixture, demo mode, or performance shortcut that bypasses governed execution for effects must fail.

negative_tests.title: Handoff treated as execution approval
negative_tests.assertion: A prepared handoff marked approved must not be accepted as Runtime decision approval.

negative_tests.title: Handoff missing review or heat
negative_tests.assertion: A handoff lacking adversarial review or current heat profile must be rejected.

negative_tests.title: Missing selected branch
negative_tests.assertion: A governed effect request without exactly one selected branch must fail closed.

negative_tests.title: Missing tenant actor governance correlation
negative_tests.assertion: A request missing tenant, actor, governance, or correlation context must fail closed.

negative_tests.title: Speculative branch to effect eligibility
negative_tests.assertion: A Proposed, Materialized, Simulating, Archived, Pruned, or Aborted branch cannot become effect eligible.

negative_tests.title: Loser branch to execution candidate
negative_tests.assertion: A CollapsedLoser branch cannot become effect eligible without rematerialization as new lineage.

negative_tests.title: Collapse without evaluated alternatives
negative_tests.assertion: Collapse must reject candidate sets without evaluated alternatives.

negative_tests.title: Multiple winners
negative_tests.assertion: A collapse record with more than one winner must be invalid.

negative_tests.title: Silent loser deletion
negative_tests.assertion: Loser/pruned/aborted branch history cannot be silently deleted after evaluation.

negative_tests.title: Candidate without adversarial review
negative_tests.assertion: Execution-candidate preparation must reject missing adversarial review state.

negative_tests.title: Perspective review freeform only
negative_tests.assertion: Perspective review that emits only prose without typed finding fields must not satisfy structured review claims.

negative_tests.title: Refinement mutates original branch
negative_tests.assertion: Refinement must not overwrite the original branch snapshot.

negative_tests.title: Unbounded refinement loop
negative_tests.assertion: Refinement must halt at configured max iterations or critical heat.

negative_tests.title: Counter-plan directly selected
negative_tests.assertion: A contradiction counter-plan cannot be selected as an execution candidate without rematerialization through Temporal Echo.

negative_tests.title: High confidence bypasses governance
negative_tests.assertion: High confidence cannot override missing anchors, critical heat, policy denial, or receipt requirements.

negative_tests.title: Confidence without calibration version
negative_tests.assertion: Confidence profiles without calibration/version metadata are insufficient for verification.

negative_tests.title: Critical heat proceeds silently
negative_tests.assertion: Critical heat must trigger block, prune, pause, or escalation before handoff.

negative_tests.title: Composite heat collapses dimensions
negative_tests.assertion: Composite heat cannot erase dimensional contributor traceability.

negative_tests.title: Oversight mode lowers heat sensitivity
negative_tests.assertion: Lower human touchpoint oversight cannot reduce heat sensitivity or challenge rigor.

negative_tests.title: Route receipt policy evidence tamper
negative_tests.assertion: Changing roleId, sessionContextClass, localityRequirement, minimumModelFloor, allowedProviders, or policyDerivationVersion must invalidate route receipt verification.

negative_tests.title: Routing missing policy commitment
negative_tests.assertion: A route receipt without policy evidence commitment cannot satisfy governed routing proof.

negative_tests.title: Provider fallback violates locality
negative_tests.assertion: Fallback must not route local-only work to remote-only providers or the reverse.

negative_tests.title: Provider fallback violates floor
negative_tests.assertion: Fallback must not select a model below minimum floor or outside allowed provider set.

negative_tests.title: Routing success grants execution
negative_tests.assertion: Successful route selection must not produce execution authority, Runtime approval, MCP authority, Cortex write authority, or signing authority.

negative_tests.title: Density artifact authorizes execution
negative_tests.assertion: Perspective, refinement, graph, confidence, or contradiction artifacts cannot authorize execution.

negative_tests.title: Mutation removes non-authority guard
negative_tests.assertion: Mutation scenarios that remove non-authority guards must fail proof-pack verification.

negative_tests.title: Reasoning graph missing lineage
negative_tests.assertion: Reasoning graph construction without intent and branch lineage must be invalid.

negative_tests.title: Reasoning graph replaces receipt
negative_tests.assertion: Reasoning graph interpretation cannot override receipts or causal anchors.

negative_tests.title: Witness narrative free-floating
negative_tests.assertion: Witness narrative/digest lacking branch, collapse, heat, participant, or receipt/causal anchors must be invalid.

negative_tests.title: Witness conflicts with receipt
negative_tests.assertion: When witness narrative conflicts with receipt-grounded truth, receipts must win.

negative_tests.title: Causal record missing required anchors
negative_tests.assertion: Collective causal record envelope missing intent, branch, collapse, review, heat, or lineage refs must fail.

negative_tests.title: Causal record outranks receipts
negative_tests.assertion: Causal records and reconstructive anchors cannot supersede receipt truth.

negative_tests.title: Memory interpretation outranks receipt
negative_tests.assertion: Memory or summary interpretation cannot override receipt-grounded truth.

negative_tests.title: Oversight profile reduces governance floor
negative_tests.assertion: No oversight profile may reduce immutable governance floor.

negative_tests.title: Observational mode reduces safeguards
negative_tests.assertion: Observational or low-touch mode must compensate with richer machine-side observability/challenge, not less governance.

negative_tests.title: Agent self-authorizes wider scope
negative_tests.assertion: Agent or role cannot widen its inherited tenant, actor, or governance scope.

negative_tests.title: Temporary elevation bypasses boundary
negative_tests.assertion: Gradient authority temporary elevation cannot bypass Runtime/Gateway governance.

negative_tests.title: Silent role rebirth
negative_tests.assertion: Agent/role lifecycle changes must preserve lineage and cannot silently replace persistent role identity.

negative_tests.title: Dream offering executes
negative_tests.assertion: Dream offerings cannot execute, mutate external state, call external systems, or invoke Runtime/Gateway effects.

negative_tests.title: Dream crosses tenant boundary
negative_tests.assertion: Tenant-scoped dreams cannot leak to other tenants or system memory without promotion policy.

negative_tests.title: Tenant memory cross-leak
negative_tests.assertion: Tenant memory retrieval must not return another tenant's content.

negative_tests.title: Raw tenant data in system memory
negative_tests.assertion: System memory must reject raw tenant-confidential content.

negative_tests.title: Promotion without approval receipts
negative_tests.assertion: Cross-tenant promoted learning requires sanitization, generalization, policy approval, and promotion receipts.

negative_tests.title: Collective API executes effects
negative_tests.assertion: Collective API/service endpoints must not become execution boundary or mutate external reality directly.

negative_tests.title: Proof pack undocumented
negative_tests.assertion: A proof pack claim without manifest discovery row and runnable verification path is insufficient.

negative_tests.title: Proof claim without negative evidence
negative_tests.assertion: Proof claims backed only by happy-path tests or docs must be downgraded or marked false.

negative_tests.title: Unsupported CAES conformance claim
negative_tests.assertion: CAES-conformant or Level 1/2/3 claims without complete conformance statement must be false_claim.

negative_tests.title: Runtime governance reimplemented in Collective
negative_tests.assertion: Collective must not implement its own authoritative Decide-before-Execute policy engine.

negative_tests.title: Cortex canonical memory reimplemented in Collective
negative_tests.assertion: Collective must not claim ownership of Cortex canonical memory truth or derivative vector internals.

negative_tests.title: Strategic feature claimed shipped
negative_tests.assertion: Features marked strategic-architecture or positioning-reviewed must not be claimed shipped without implementation/test evidence.

negative_tests.title: Contract implementation without validators
negative_tests.assertion: If canonical contract implementation is claimed, missing shared interfaces or invariant validator tests must fail.

negative_tests.title: Orchestration lane contamination
negative_tests.assertion: Round work that stacks lanes, reuses worktrees, touches unrelated code, or lacks receipts must fail orchestration review.

negative_tests.title: Forbidden copy claims
negative_tests.assertion: Copy implying Collective is ungoverned, executes directly, replaces Runtime/Gateway, or makes narratives equal to receipts must fail.

false_claim_gates.gate: False unless the repo proves every effect crosses MCP Gateway and Runtime Decide-before-Execute, and copy clearly states Collective prepares candidates only.
False unless branch state model, collapse record, evaluated alternatives, loser preservation, and negative transition tests exist.
False unless adversarial review state is required before effect-candidate preparation and missing-review negative tests pass.

false_claim_gates.claim: Cognitive Density proof pack is implemented
false_claim_gates.gate: False unless positive, negative, and mutation scenarios prove perspective/refinement/graph/confidence/contradiction artifacts are non-authority.

false_claim_gates.claim: Governed role model routing is implemented
false_claim_gates.gate: False unless route receipt policy evidence commitments are present, tamper tests pass, and fallback preserves locality/floor/allowed-provider policy.
False unless narratives are anchored to causal/receipt refs and subordinate to receipts.
False unless system, tenant, session, and promoted-learning scopes are enforced with tenant isolation and promotion policy tests.
False unless a complete CAES conformance statement exists. Otherwise only CAES-aligned defensibility may be claimed.
False unless tenant-scoped API/service boundary exists with tests proving it remains cognition-only and does not execute effects.
False unless quiescence/proposal generation exists and dream artifacts are non-effecting with tenant boundary tests.

remediation_lanes.name: Plane separation and non-bypass gates
remediation_lanes.success_condition: Static and test gates prove cognition cannot execute or bypass Runtime/MCP Gateway.

remediation_lanes.name: Temporal Echo and collapse lineage
remediation_lanes.success_condition: Branch lifecycle, collapse, loser preservation, and forbidden transition tests pass.

remediation_lanes.name: Adversarial challenge and Cognitive Density non-authority
remediation_lanes.success_condition: Density artifacts deepen deliberation but cannot authorize execution.

remediation_lanes.name: Heat, confidence, and oversight compensation
remediation_lanes.success_condition: Critical heat and lower-touch oversight fail safe without reducing governance rigor.

remediation_lanes.name: Routing evidence and negative corpus
remediation_lanes.success_condition: Route receipt policy evidence commitments and negative routing corpus pass deterministically.

remediation_lanes.name: Witness, truth, causal record envelope
remediation_lanes.success_condition: Narratives and causal records are anchored and subordinate to receipts.

remediation_lanes.name: Memory planes, dreams, lifecycle
remediation_lanes.success_condition: Roles preserve lineage, dreams are non-effecting, and tenant memory scopes do not leak.

remediation_lanes.name: Service/API and integration boundaries
remediation_lanes.success_condition: Collective is a tenant-scoped cognition service and handoffs remain candidates only.

remediation_lanes.name: Proof manifest, CAES wording, copy gates
remediation_lanes.success_condition: Proof/copy claims match implementation evidence and CAES language is correctly gated.

remediation_lanes.name: Canonical contracts and orchestration hygiene
remediation_lanes.success_condition: C# contracts, validators, and round orchestration rules are evidence-backed.

verdict_policy.conformant: All critical and high requirements implemented with direct evidence, all required negative tests pass, proof/copy claims match evidence, and no forbidden cross-boundary execution paths exist.
verdict_policy.conditionally_conformant: No critical failures or false claims; some medium/low requirements are partial, documented-only, or ambiguous with remediation lanes identified.
verdict_policy.non_conformant: Any critical failure, unsupported execution-authority claim, direct cognition-to-effect path, missing non-authority guard, missing tenant isolation for memory scopes, unsupported CAES conformance claim, or false proof claim.
